title: springy injera bowl
description: an indulgent ethiopian-inspired bowl featuring tender snow peas, crispy shallots, and a rich butter sauce, all atop a light and airy injera cradle, perfect for a pescatarian-friendly lunch. this recipe showcases the unique texture and flavor of injera, paired with the freshness of snow peas and the crunch of toasted shallots.
instructions: heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
add the sliced shallots and cook, stirring frequently, until they are golden brown and crispy, about 5 minutes.
remove the shallots from the skillet with a slotted spoon and set them aside on a paper towel-lined plate.
add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
add the ground cardamom and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the snow peas to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until they are tender but still crisp, about 3-4 minutes.
remove the snow peas from the skillet with a slotted spoon and set them aside with the shallots.
add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the skillet and stir until melted.
add the water to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 minutes.
add the cooked snow peas and shallots back to the skillet and stir to combine.
season with salt and black pepper to taste.
divide the injera into wedges and place 1-2 wedges in the bottom of each copper mug.
add a spoonful of the snow pea and shallot mixture on top of the injera.
